findings.description: Job payload fields source/dest are interpolated into a shell string executed with shell=True, allowing arbitrary command injection from untrusted job data.
attempt is reset to 0 on every loop iteration, so a persistently failing job retries forever (run_with_retries never returns False) and the backoff multiplier never escalates past the first step.
Blocking time.sleep inside the async poll_loop coroutine stalls the entire event loop and prevents task cancellation; it should be await asyncio.sleep.
subprocess.run has no timeout, so a hung convert process blocks the worker indefinitely.
run_with_retries performs blocking sleeps and subprocess calls synchronously inside the async poll loop, starving other coroutines; it should be offloaded via asyncio.to_thread or an executor.
values from the payload is summed without validation; non-numeric or non-iterable values raise TypeError, which the retry wrapper then treats as a transient, retryable failure.
Even with shell injection fixed, passing unvalidated paths to ImageMagick convert permits abuse of coder pseudo-protocols (e.g. msl: or pipe-prefixed filenames) absent path allowlisting.
list.pop(0) is O(n) per dequeue; collections.deque with popleft would be O(1).
The backoff sleep executes unconditionally after the final attempt as well, adding pointless delay before the function gives up.
stop_after is compared against cumulative lifetime processed+failed counters, so a reused worker instance exits prematurely instead of counting only jobs handled in this call.
Permanent failures (unknown job kind, malformed payload) are retried identically to transient ones; there is no retryable vs non-retryable distinction.
json.dumps raises TypeError for non-JSON-serializable payload values, turning a simple echo job into a retried failure.
Missing payload validation lets source/dest default to empty strings, producing a meaningless convert command that is then pointlessly retried.
The full job payload is logged at INFO level, which can leak sensitive data such as secrets or PII into logs.
drain_sync returns cumulative lifetime processed/failed totals rather than counts for this drain, which is misleading on repeated calls.
The check-then-pop expression is not thread-safe; if the queue is shared across threads a concurrent consumer can empty the list between the check and pop, raising IndexError.
asyncio is imported but never used, which is symptomatic of the missing await asyncio.sleep in poll_loop.
stdout/stderr are captured but never logged, discarding diagnostics for failed convert jobs.
Public methods execute_job and run_with_retries lack docstrings.
Mixes typing.Optional with PEP 585 builtin generics; could standardize on the X | None style.
